steps.title: Identify regrowth evidence
steps.content: The passage says reforms and falling meat prices triggered natural regrowth by the mid-1980s. The graph evidence must therefore show forest cover increasing after the deforestation period.

steps.title: Reject non-growth evidence
steps.content: Choice B compares two classes in one year, which does not show regrowth. Choice C also compares classes in one year. Choice D shows forest cover decreasing from 1960 to 1979, which describes deforestation, not recovery.

steps.title: Use the recovery trend
steps.content: The remaining choice shows forest cover increasing for all classes from 1979 to 2000, directly supporting the claim of natural regrowth. The answer is A.
